concerns.text: Task T022 specifies a 'subset of 500 tasks' for dynamic score generation. This directly violates spec.md US-2 and FR-002 which mandate a subset of '50 tasks' to satisfy the 5-hour runtime constraint (SC-003). This is a 10x relaxation of the resource constraint, likely causing the pipeline to exceed the hard time limit.

Task T031 defines the permutation test with a 'hard timeout (5.5h)' and 'early stopping' based on p-value stability. This relaxes spec.md FR-005 which mandates a specific range of 'up to 10,000 iterations (minimum 5,000)'. Relying on time-based early stopping risks failing to meet the minimum iteration count required for statistical significance defined in the spec.

Task T032 introduces a 'Linear Mixed-Effects Model (LMM)' as a secondary validation method. This scope is not present in spec.md FR-004 or SC-005. Introducing unverified statistical methods violates the constraint that tasks must not silently relax or expand the spec's defined methodology without explicit authorization.

Task T033 requires implementing a 'fine-tuned BERT' classifier for reasoning patterns. The spec.md Assumptions section explicitly states the study will 'proceed with the available token-level data' and does not authorize training external models. This violates Constitution Principle VII (Resource-Constrained Methodology) and introduces an unverified dependency not in the spec.

Task T016 describes implementing the StaticScorer class but fails to explicitly mandate the 'epsilon smoothing factor of 1e-9' required by spec.md Edge Cases to handle log(0) scenarios. Omitting this critical failure-mode handling in the task description risks a silent violation of the spec's robustness requirements.

Task T030 implements Pearson and Spearman correlation but omits the 'Ljung-Box test for autocorrelation' required by spec.md SC-005 to validate the residual error distribution. This is a dropped acceptance criterion that leaves the residual analysis incomplete per the spec.
